args.thought: The user wants me to search for good stocks that will possibly grow and create a growth portfolio for potential profit in 3 months. I need to break this down into steps: 1) Define what makes a stock suitable for short-term growth (3-month horizon), 2) Identify criteria for screening such stocks, 3) Use stock_screener to find candidates, 4) Validate with recent performance/news, 5) Suggest a diversified portfolio allocation. I should start by thinking about appropriate screening criteria for short-term growth potential.

For a 3-month growth portfolio, I should focus on stocks with strong near-term catalysts: recent earnings beats, positive analyst revisions, high relative strength, and growth in key metrics. Screening criteria: revenue growth >15% YoY, EPS growth >20% YoY, RSI between 50-70 (not overbought), average volume >1M, and price >$5 to avoid penny stocks. I'll use stock_screener with these criteria.

content: I'll continue with the analysis to identify suitable growth stocks for a 3-month portfolio. Let me screen for stocks meeting our growth criteria.

args.query: stocks with revenue growth greater than 15% year over year, EPS growth greater than 20% year over year, RSI between 50 and 70, average volume greater than 1 million, price greater than $5

Stock: AMD (Advanced Micro Devices Inc)

Quarter: Q4 FY25 Result: Beat consensus significantly on all key headline reported metrics however the result is messy. Headline Revenue of $10.27B beat expectations by ~6.2%, and Adjusted EPS of $1.53 beat by ~15.9%.  ...    Growth Momentum: Headline revenue grew 34.1% YoY and 11.1% QoQ. The annualized QoQ rate however is unreliable to extrapolate with AMD a seasonal business in the non data center segments. Data Center revenue grew 39% YoY and 24% QoQ, confirming the rapid ramp of the MI300/MI400 AI accelerator series. Client and Gaming revenues showed strong year over year growth (see table) however this was a sequential decline and  ...    MD&A Tone: Highly Optimistic. Management shifted from “outstanding” progress in Q3 to describing 2025 as a “defining  ...    Guidance: Above Consensus. Q1 FY26 revenue guidance of $9.8B (+/- $300M) is ~2%-8% above street expectations (low to  ...    segment they see revenue growing at 60% annually over the same timeframe and the AI business being “tens of billions”  ...    The MI450 ramp will be 2H loaded, particularly towards Q4 2026. Analyst Impact: Likely Upgrades. The combination of a beat-and-raise quarter, record free cash flow, and strong forward  ...    driven by R&D evident. Conclusion: AMD is executing well in the Data Center pivot but each quarter there are a lot of moving parts. While the Q4 gross margin beat (57%) was aided by a $360M inventory reversal, the underlying margin of ~55% and revenue guidance suggest the core business is healthier than consensus modeled.  ...    of operating expenses for Q1 however, the call reported this to be expected at $3.05bn. The company is effectively establishing itself as the clear secondary source to the market leader in AI compute and growth is strong, however clean earnings beats and revenue growth acceleration remain elusive. Author’s note: From an investor perspective taking into account AMD’s elevated forward P/E of 43x, and recent  ...    AMD delivered a record-breaking quarter, driven by rapid growth in Data Center demand. Note that Q4 Non-GAAP Gross  ...    Data Center revenue ramp: Revenue hit a record $5.4B, up 39% YoY, driven by strong demand for EPYC processors and  ...    Management Discussion (Current Quarter - Q4 FY25): CEO Lisa Su characterized 2025 as a “defining year” with record  ...    Management provided Q1 FY26 guidance that exceeds current market consensus, suggesting that the AI ramp is accelerating  ...    Note: Full year FY26 guidance was not explicitly provided in the extracted snippets, limiting the table to Q1. Implied Growth: The Q1 revenue guide of $9.8B implies 32% YoY growth, roughly maintaining the high-growth pace set in  ...    Revenues will be upgraded but higher expenses and non data center softness will mute the extent of earnings upgrades. Acceleration in the data center segment however will be seen as a greater positive than the offsetting sequential  ...    ---

### Conclusion AMD has successfully transitioned from “promising AI competitor” to the clear secondary source of compute for AI data centers. Its launch of the MI400-450 range later this year with the indicated performance specs, coupled with deals with OpenAI and others support this development and continued strong growth in the high margin data center segment. The Q4 results however were mixed, with revenues strong but normalized earnings in line. While the massive earnings beat was aided by a $360M inventory reversal, the core business health is undeniable: Data Center revenue is accelerating (+39% YoY), and the company is guiding Q1 FY26 revenue nearly 5% above consensus.
